officer 0.7.4.006
  • Function reference
  • User Documentation

Add an image to an Excel sheet

Source: R/read_xlsx.R
sheet_add_drawing.external_img.Rd

Add an image file (PNG, JPEG, GIF, ...) to a sheet in an xlsx workbook created with read_xlsx(). The image is copied into xl/media/ and placed on the sheet via an absolute anchor (inch-based position and size).

Usage

# S3 method for class 'external_img'
sheet_add_drawing(
  x,
  value,
  sheet,
  left = 1,
  top = 1,
  width = NULL,
  height = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

x

rxlsx object (created by read_xlsx())

value

an external_img() object

sheet

sheet name (must already exist)

left, top

top-left anchor of the image, in inches. Defaults to (1, 1).

width, height

size of the image, in inches. When NULL (default) the dimensions stored on value (from external_img()) are used.

...

unused

Value

the rxlsx object (invisibly)

See also

sheet_add_drawing(), external_img()

Examples

img <- system.file("extdata", "example.png", package = "officer")
if (nzchar(img) && file.exists(img)) {
  x <- read_xlsx()
  x <- add_sheet(x, label = "pics")
  x <- sheet_add_drawing(
    x, sheet = "pics",
    value = external_img(img, width = 2, height = 2)
  )
  print(x, target = tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx"))
}
